summary: Rule chunk from Gamemastering Paranoia page 96, chunk 1.

mechanics.raw_text:
, 
fe ee Know When to Ignore the Dice Suppose you roll the die and get a 20, and your favorite villain is dead before he has time to complete Stage 1 of his diabolical plan to destroy Life, the Universe, and Everything So much for the nifly adventure you've Carelully planned out ‘Ate yOu really going fo waste al the work you put into preparing that adventure, just because of a stupid dieoll? Pointedly ignore the dice when you want to remind the players that There Ain't No lustice, Judicious and infrequent displays of arrogant. indifference to die-rolls lets. the players know that the rules can’t save them: "Oh, Should ! have given you an agility roll to duck out of the way of the runaway algae harvester? Well, excuuuse me. (Roll dice. Don't look at them.) Oh, Too bad. You failed the roll, That’s the breaks. You know what they say — the dice don't lie, and neither does, ‘The Computer. Now, ready to continue?” Paranoia GMs are supposed to be arbitrary and perverse, Alpha Complex is a horrible place to live. A friendly reminder now and then helps the players adopt that cheer, ‘carefree resignation to their doom that is the soul of Paranoia’s unique and charming atmosphere. Roll Dice A Lot Find plenty of excuses to roll dice. Players love to hear rolling dice, even when the e fect on theie characters is negligible, obscure or illusory When you don't need to conceal the result, let the players roll, Attribute and skill lls can always be tossed in on impulse ~ "Say, give me an endurance roll, You must be getting, tired of lugging that Semi-Portable Specimen, Freezer Unit around.” ‘You should always be rolling dice where the players can't read the results. Don’t tll anyone.
